title: Klaviyo vs Drip (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Klaviyo and Drip email marketing platforms. See pricing, features, and which tool wins for ecommerce.
h1: Klaviyo vs Drip: Detailed Comparison for E-Commerce Email Marketing
intro: Both Klaviyo and Drip are purpose-built email marketing platforms designed for e-commerce businesses. This comparison will help you decide which platform offers better value, features, and integration capabilities for your store, whether you're just starting out or scaling revenue.

comparison_table.tool_a: Freemium with free tier (up to 500 contacts)

comparison_table.tool_b: Deep store integration and revenue tracking

comparison_table.tool_a: Flows, predictive analytics, segmentation, SMS, reviews
comparison_table.tool_b: Visual automation builder, revenue attribution, onsite campaigns, SMS

choose_a_if: You want a free tier to test email marketing without upfront costs
You need predictive analytics and AI-powered insights for campaigns
You're building on Shopify or WooCommerce and want seamless integration
choose_b_if: You prioritize lower entry-level pricing starting at $39/month
You need detailed revenue attribution and e-commerce tracking
You want to earn an affiliate commission for referring the platform

faq.a: Klaviyo edges ahead with a free tier, better G2 rating (4.6 vs 4.4), superior predictive analytics, and stronger Shopify integration. However, Drip excels at revenue attribution and starts $6 cheaper per month. The best choice depends on whether you prioritize cost savings or advanced features.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Klaviyo or Drip?
faq.a: Drip has a lower entry price at $39/month versus Klaviyo's $45/month. However, Klaviyo's free tier accommodates up to 500 contacts, making it cheaper for small businesses testing email marketing. For established stores, Drip's slightly lower pricing may provide better value.

faq.q: Can I switch from Drip to Klaviyo?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support contact list imports, making migration straightforward. You can export your subscriber data from Drip and import it into Klaviyo while preserving segments and custom fields. Plan your automation rebuilds in advance, as flows and workflows will need to be recreated in the new platform.
